description: A vertical, portrait-orientation promotional graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor advertising apprenticeships. The background is a full-color photograph of a hands-on training scene: a young Black man in the foreground wearing clear protective safety goggles and a dark navy shirt looks downward in concentration, while at right an older white man, also in clear safety goggles and a dark shirt, leans in as a mentor; industrial/lab equipment (a yellow upright apparatus, tubing, a metal canister) is visible, conveying a workshop or technical-training environment. The lower portion fades to a deep navy gradient. Overlaid in large bold white sans-serif text is the slogan on two lines: 'Earn as you learn with apprenticeship,' and below it, in white, the URL 'apprenticeship.gov.' The white embossed/outline Department of Labor circular seal ('Department of Labor, United States of America') appears at the lower right. The style is a clean recruitment poster pairing a relatable mentorship photo with a catchy rhyming slogan. The messaging promotes registered apprenticeships (tied to #MentoringMonth) as a way to earn wages while gaining skills, supporting the administration's workforce-development agenda. Target audience is prospective apprentices/young workers.
notable_text: Earn as you learn with apprenticeship - apprenticeship.gov
